Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Complaint; And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (by Zoom):
Andrew Williams, Esq. 305-916-1122 The Williams Law Group for Plaintiff.
Nima Masjedi Zadeh, Esq. 415-926-7600 KAUFMAN DOLOWICH LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT SANDBOX VR, INC.'s Motion To Dismiss Complaint; And Request For Sanctions.

The court adopts the tentative ruling. In addition, the depositions will take place on 4/16/25 and 4/21/25 and parties to move for a trial continuance.

Defendant Sandbox VR's motion to dismiss and request for sanctions is denied in its entirety. Sandbox fails to show that any prejudice it claims to have suffered as a result of the asserted misconduct and neglect of plaintiffs and their counsel could not be remedied by a trial continuance (which, despite plaintiffs' agreement to continue the trial, it never sought) or a discovery motion such as a motion to compel further responses or to compel attendance at a deposition. Nor has Sandbox identified any portion of any order that plaintiffs violated. Nor has Sandbox shown that any of the asserted misconduct and neglect was due to any cause other than the family issues of plaintiffs' counsel and/or a hurricane and thus there is no showing that any misconduct or neglect committed by plaintiffs and their counsel was willful and inexcusable. There is thus no basis to impose the extraordinary sanction of dismissal or even a lesser sanction or penalty of requiring a monetary payment.

Mr. Williams to submit a proposed form of order that is verbatim to the court's ruling.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)
